rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the intended component type or implementation scope
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for port-related assumptions and guarantees
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting annex blocks
Use appropriate scope for assumptions and guarantees based on the component type or implementation context
Preserve existing connections and subcomponents when adding implementation-level bindings
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes for port-related contracts
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
Do not place assumptions and guarantees in invalid scopes
Do not mix component type and implementation-level bindings in the same annex block
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax for assume, guarantee, assign, and validator diagnostics
